content.problemStatement:
Chef has a beautiful garden that contains NNN flowers. Initially, all the flowers are in full bloom. However, each flower is a bit shy – once plucked, the ithi_{th}ith​ flower takes exactly TiT_iTi​ days to regrow and bloom again.

Chef loves plucking flowers but also wants his garden to always look beautiful. For this reason, he insists that there are always at least KKK flowers blooming in the garden at any given time.

Over a period of DDD days (numbered from 111 to DDD inclusive), Chef will pluck some of the blooming flowers. On any day, he can choose to pluck any number of blooming flowers as long as after plucking, there remain at least KKK blooming flowers.

Note that:


If ithi_{th}ith​ flower is plucked on day XXX, it will regrow and be available again on day X+TiX + T_iX+Ti​.
The same flower may be plucked multiple times, provided that it has regrown after a previous pluck.
Determine the maximum total number of times Chef can pluck a flower in DDD days while respecting the garden’s beauty requirement.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, KKK and DDD — the number of flowers in the garden, the minimum number of flowers that must always bloom, and the number of days over which Chef plucks flowers, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers T1,T2,…,TNT_1, T_2, \dots, T_NT1​,T2​,…,TN​, where TiT_iTi​ represents the number of days the ithi_{th}ith​ flower takes to regrow after being plucked.




Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer — the maximum number of times Chef can pluck a flower in DDD days.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : You can keep on plucking the 1st1^{st}1st flower every day to get a total of 333 flowers over the 333 days.
Note that the flower regrows in 111 day, so it can be plucked on the next day. Also, we cannot pluck more than 111 flower a day, because we need to keep at least 222 flowers blooming.
Hence, the answer is 333.
Test Case 2 : Over 101010 days, Chef never plucks any flowers because if he were to pluck even one, he would violate the constraint of having at least 222 blooming flowers at all times.
